title: 6G-oriented visible light communication system and method
abstract: The invention relates to a 6G-oriented visible light communication system and method. The visible light communication system comprises an optical interconnection device, a plurality of visible light emitting devices and a plurality of visible light receiving devices, wherein the optical interconnection device comprises an optical router, a plurality of wave separators and a plurality of wave combiners; the optical router is respectively connected with the wave separators and the wave combiners, the plurality of wave separators are connected with the plurality of visible light emitting devices one by one, and the plurality of wave combiners are connected with the plurality of visible light receiving devices one by one. Thus, routing and distribution of parallel optical signals are efficiently controlled through the optical interconnection device, and wavelength division multiplexing of visible light communication is realized through the optical interconnection device, the visible light

title: METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR TRANSMISSION OF DOWNLINK SIGNAL FOR INITIAL ACCESS IN A WIRELESS COMMUNICATION SYSTEM
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5G communication system or a 6G communication system for supporting higher data rates beyond a 4G communication system such as long term evolution (LTE). According to embodiments of the disclosure, a synchronization signal block (SSB) including a primary synchronization signal (PSS), a secondary synchronization signal (SSS), a physical broadcast channel (PBCH) may be transmitted using first and second beams. One or more symbols adjoining the first and second synchronization signals may be configured to accommodate a beam switching time.

title: METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR BEAM-SPECIFIC DOWNLINK/UPLINK OPERATION IN A WIRELESS COMMUNICATION SYSTEM
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5G communication system or a 6G communication system for supporting higher data rates beyond a 4G communication system such as long term evolution (LTE). A method performed by a user equipment (UE) in a wireless communication system is provided. The method comprises receiving a configuration for at least one of a downlink (DL) reception or an uplink (UL) transmission, wherein the configuration information comprises beam identifiers (IDs) and a slot and symbol configuration for respective ones of the one or more beam IDs. The method further includes performing, according to the configuration information, a DL operation or an UL operation.

title: METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR TRANSMISSION of DOWNLINK SIGNAL FOR INITIAL ACCESS IN A WIRELESS COMMUNICATION SYSTEM
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate than a 4G communication system such as LTE. According to an embodiment of the present disclosure, a synchronization signal block (SSB) including a primary synchronization signal (PSS), a secondary synchronization signal (SSS), and a physical broadcast channel (PBCH) can be transmitted by using a first beam and a second beam. In addition, one or more symbols adjacent to a first synchronization signal and a second synchronization signal may be configured to accommodate a beam switching time.

title: Robust energy efficiency optimization method for intelligent metasurface-assisted wireless power supply network
abstract: The invention relates to a robust energy efficiency optimization method for an intelligent metasurface-assisted wireless power supply network, and belongs to the technical field of 6G wireless resource management. The method comprises the following steps: S1, building an intelligent metasurface-assisted multi-user wireless power supply communication network scene energy and information transmission model; s2, considering a user energy consumption constraint, a transmission time constraint, a minimum throughput constraint, an energy station maximum transmitting power constraint and an intelligent metasurface reflection phase shift constraint, and establishing a multi-user wireless power supply communication network energy efficiency maximization robust resource allocation model based on bounded channel uncertainty intelligent metasurface assistance; s3, converting an objective function into a two-item subtraction form, and converting an original optimization problem into a deterministic

title: Cobalt-free positive electrode material precursor and preparation method thereof
assignee: HUNAN SHANSHAN ENERGY TECH CO LTD
abstract: The invention discloses a cobalt-free positive electrode material precursor. Nickel manganese hydroxide is taken as a matrix, and the surface of the matrix is coated with a zirconium hydroxide layer. The preparation method comprises the following steps of: preparing a mixed salt solution containing nickel, manganese and doped elements; adding pure water as a base solution into a reaction kettle, and then adding ammonia water to control the concentration of the ammonia water in the base solution to be 2-6g/L; introducing nitrogen into the reaction kettle, adding the mixed salt solution, a precipitator and ammonia water into the reaction kettle, and stirring for reaction; and finally, adding ZrSO4 and (NH4)2SO4 into the reaction kettle for continued reaction, and carrying out filtering, washing and drying after the reaction is finished, so as to obtain the cobalt-free positive electrode material precursor. title: Multi-antenna unmanned aerial vehicle network deployment parameter setting method under 6G
abstract: Due to the fact that the unmanned aerial vehicle large-probability line-of-sight link improves the communication link quality, strong interference is brought to users of adjacent cells. Therefore, the invention provides a multi-antenna unmanned aerial vehicle network deployment parameter setting method under 6G. The method specifically comprises the following steps: firstly, determining a deployment center and a deployment height of an unmanned aerial vehicle base station group according to environmental parameters, and determining a range of unmanned aerial vehicle base stations serving ground users; secondly, introducing a multi-antenna model, and calculating the power intensity of interference signals and useful signals through gamma approximation; then based on the useful and interference signal intensity of the multi-antenna unmanned aerial vehicle network, obtaining an average traversal rate of ground users in a network coverage range, and exploring a change condition of the aver

title: METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR DOWNLINK OFDMA HAVING DFT-PRECODING APPLIED THERETO
abstract: The disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transfer rate beyond a 4G communication system, such as LTE. A method by a base station in a communication system according to an embodiment may include: determining the number of DFT-precoding chunks on which DFT precoding is performed; determining a power backoff value of a power amplifier (PA) of the base station; transmitting information indicating the number of DFT-precoding chunks to a terminal; transmitting downlink control information (DCI) including a resource allocation field, configured based on the number of DFT-precoding chunks, to the terminal; and transmitting data to the terminal according to the resource allocation field included in the DCI.

abstract: The invention provides a coupled feed broadband MIMO antenna assembly and a mobile terminal. The coupled feed broadband MIMO antenna assembly comprises a rectangular metal floor and a dielectric frame arranged around the edge of the metal floor. Each long edge of the dielectric frame is provided with two MIMO antenna units; and the MIMO antenna unit comprises a feed patch and a coupling strip, and the feed patch and the coupling strip are located on the two opposite faces of the dielectric frame respectively. The four MIMO antenna units are arranged on the dielectric frame, so that the antenna assembly has relatively good radiation performance in a Sub-6G frequency band range; besides, the feed patches and the coupling strips in the MIMO antenna units are respectively arranged on the two opposite surfaces of the dielectric frame, so that better coupling is generated between the feed patch and the coupling strip, and the bandwidth of the antenna is expanded. title: COLUMNAR SEMICONDUCTOR DEVICE, AND METHOD FOR MANUFACTURING SAME
abstract: In an SRAM cell, an Si column 6a of a selected SGT of an array of upper-level Si columns 6a to 6e is at the left end in the X-direction. An Si column 6j of a selected SGT of an array of lower-level Si column rows 6f to 6j is at the right end. In a plan view, a lower-level Si column 6g is present in the width of a contact hole 47a extended in the Y-direction. In the plan view, an upper-level Si column 6d is present in the width of the contact hole 47b extended in the Y-direction. In the upper level, a TiN layer 40b providing gate electrodes of a load SGT and a drive SGT is formed in contact with a side surface of an entire gate region vertically between Si columns 6c, 6d, and 6e. Similarly, in the lower level, a TiN layer 40c providing gate electrodes of the load SGT and the drive SGT is formed in contact with a side surface of an entire gate region vertically between the Si columns 6f, 6g, and 6h.

title: DFT-precoding OFDMA METHOD AND PPARATUS FOR DOWNLINK OFDMA WITH DFT-PRECODING
abstract: The present invention relates to a 5G or a 6G communication system to support higher data transmission rates after a 4G communication system such as LTE. An objective of the present invention is to properly adjust the output power of a power amplifier (PA) by adjusting power backoff. According to one embodiment of the present invention, a method of a base station of a communication system comprises: a step of determining the number of DFT-precoding chunks on which DFT precoding is performed; a step of determining a power backoff value of a power amplifier (PA) of the base station; a step of transmitting information indicating the number of DFT-precoding chunks to a terminal; a step of transmitting downlink control information (DCI) including a resource allocation field constructed based on the number of DFT-precoding chunks to the terminal; and a step of transmitting data to the terminal in accordance with the resource allocation field included in the DCI.

abstract: The embodiment of the utility model discloses a virtual reality equipment control system based on a WIFI (Wireless Fidelity) 6 router. title: Virtual reality equipment management system based on WIFI (Wireless Fidelity) 6 router
abstract: The embodiment of the utility model discloses a virtual reality equipment management system based on a WIFI (Wireless Fidelity) 6 router. The system comprises a server, at least one sixth-generation wireless network technology WIFI 6 router and at least one virtual reality equipment, each virtual reality device is connected with a corresponding target WIFI 6 router in each WIFI 6 router, and is used for sending the collected user positioning data to the corresponding target WIFI 6 router; each WIFI6 router is connected with the server, and when each WIFI6 router is used as a target WIFI6 router, each WIFI6 router is used for sending each piece of user positioning data to the server; and the server is used for generating a user display image according to the user positioning data, and sending the user display image to the corresponding virtual reality equipment through the target WIFI 6 routers. title: Broadband MIMO antenna assembly and mobile terminal
assignee: KUNSHAN RUIXIANG XUNTONG COMM TECH CO LTD
abstract: The invention provides a broadband MIMO antenna assembly and a mobile terminal. The broadband MIMO antenna assembly comprises a dielectric plate, a metal floor and antenna units, wherein the metal floor and the antenna units are respectively located on two surfaces of the dielectric plate; the antenna units are T-shaped, and the number of antenna units is not less than two; the metal floor is provided with a plurality of slots at the vertical projection of the antenna units, the slots are in one-to-one correspondence with the antenna units, and the slots are communicated with the edge of the metal floor. Through the design of the plurality of T-shaped antenna units, the efficiency of the antenna is relatively high; the antenna unit is matched with the slot on the metal floor, so that the isolation of the antenna is improved while the bandwidth of the antenna is effectively expanded. title: METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR POWER CONTROL FOR NETWORK ENERGY OPTIMIZATION
abstract: The disclosure relates to a 5G communication system or a 6G communication system for supporting higher data rates beyond a 4G communication system such as long term evolution (LTE). The disclosure relates to a technique for controlling a base station power using an artificial intelligence based technology to improve an energy efficiency of a communication network. A method performed by a base station of a communication system according to an embodiment of the disclosure may include acquiring state information, determining an active/sleep request indicator (ASRI) based on at least a part of the state information, transmitting at least one of the state information and the ASRI to a central unit, receiving, from the central unit, power control information determined based on the at least one of the state information and the ASRI, and performing a power control based on the received power control information.

title: Resource management and control method and system for 6G stereoscopic dense communication network
abstract: The invention discloses a resource management and control method and system for a 6G three-dimensional dense communication network, and the method comprises the steps: establishing a cooperative D2D three-dimensional dense network environment through different service demands of users, and determining the number of cellular users, the number of relay users and the number of D2D transmission pairs; in a three-dimensional dense network environment, according to D2D communication, dividing a specific communication process into two steps, establishing a corresponding optimization objective function, and solving an optimal solution by using an optimization theory; and calculating a power value corresponding to the maximum system throughput by using a Newton iteration method, comparing an absolute value of a difference between the power values obtained by two adjacent iterations with an allowable error, and ending Newton iteration operation until the absolute value of the difference between

title: Ternary precursor with porous structure and preparation method of ternary precursor
assignee: NANTONG KINGTON ENERGY STORAGE POWER NEW MAT CO LTD
abstract: The invention relates to a ternary precursor with a porous structure and a preparation method of the ternary precursor. The ternary precursor is NixCoyMnz (OH) 2, x is more than or equal to 0.70 and less than 0.98, y is more than 0 and less than 0.30, z is more than 0.01 and less than 0.30, and x + y + z = 1. title: Communication method and device
abstract: The invention provides a communication method and device, which can prevent terminal equipment from receiving unwanted paging messages so as to reduce the power consumption of the terminal equipment, and can be applied to a 4G system, a 5G system and a future communication system, such as a 6G system. The method comprises the following steps: first network equipment receives first indication information sent by terminal equipment in a connected state, and sends the first indication information to a second network equipment; when the terminal equipment is in an idle state or an inactive state, the first network equipment receives first indication information from the second network equipment and then sends a first paging message to the terminal equipment; and the terminal equipment receives the first paging message.

title: FACILITATING REAL-TIME POWER OPTIMIZATION IN ADVANCED NETWORKS
abstract: Facilitating real-time power optimization in advanced networks (e.g., 5G, 6G, and beyond) is provided herein. Operations of a method can include determining, by a system comprising a memory and a processor, a power distribution setting for a user equipment that includes multiple radios based on a historical radio power usage, a historical performance result, a current location, and an application currently executing on the user equipment. The method also can include implementing, by the system, the power distribution setting across the multiple radios of the user equipment. The first radio of the multiple radios can be a first radio type and a second radio of the multiple radios can be a second radio type, different from the first radio type.

title: Random access method and terminal
abstract: The embodiment of the invention discloses a random access method and a terminal, which are applied to the field of communication and are used for enhancing uplink coverage in 5G, 6G and other high-frequency-band communication systems, and the method comprises the steps that network device configures a plurality of SULs in a cell, wherein the plurality of SULs are used for supplementing uplink coverage of the cell; the network device sends configuration information of a plurality of SULs to the terminal device, and the terminal device can select one SUL from the plurality of SULs for random access according to the configuration information after receiving the configuration information. In the embodiments of the present application, a plurality of SULs are included in a cell, so that the demand that a large number of terminals in the cell can access a network can be satisfied, and the success rate of uplink random access of a plurality of terminals can be improved. title: Miniaturized wide-frequency-band quartering microstrip power divider
abstract: The utility model discloses a miniaturized wide-frequency-band quartering microstrip power divider, which comprises a shell (1), a signal input end (2), a first power divider (301), a second power divider (302), a third power divider (303), a first signal output end (401), a second signal output end (402), a third signal output end (403), a fourth signal output end (404), an eddy current (5) and a grounding hole (6). title: Dual-frequency antenna and electronic device thereof
assignee: USI SCIENCE AND TECH SHENZHEN CO LTD
abstract: The invention provides a dual-frequency antenna and an electronic device thereof. The dual-frequency antenna comprises a feed-in end, wherein an open hole is formed in an annular connecting end, and the annular connecting end is connected to the feed-in end; a thread head of the metal screw penetrates through the open hole, so that the metal screw is electrically connected with the annular connecting end in a conducting manner; a first extension path is connected to the feed-in end; a second extension path is connected to the first extension path; a third extension path is connected to the second extension path; and a grounding part is connected to the third extension path. title: SYSTEM AND METHOD FOR CONCATENATION AND PREPROCESSING IN DATA PLANE
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5G communication system or a 6G communication system for supporting higher data rates beyond a 4G communication system such as long term evolution (LTE). The present disclosure particularly relates to a system and method for optimizing radio link control (RLC) mechanism in data plane.

title: RANDOM ACCESS METHOD AND TERMINAL
abstract: The embodiments of the present application disclose a random access method and a terminal, which are applied to the field of communications, and used for enhancing uplink coverage in high-frequency band communication systems such as 5G and 6G. The method of the embodiments of the present application comprises: a network device configuring a plurality of SULs within a cell, the plurality of SULs being used to supplement uplink coverage of the cell; the network device sending configuration information of the plurality of SULs to a terminal device; and upon reception of the configuration information, the terminal device being able to select, according to the configuration information, one SUL from the plurality of SULs for random access. In the embodiments of the present application, a cell comprises a plurality of SULs, and can satisfy the requirements that a large number of terminals within the cell can all access a network, improving the success rate of performing uplink random access

title: Multi-server hybrid deployment architecture and method in 5G/6G edge calculation scene
abstract: The invention discloses a multi-server hybrid deployment architecture and a method in a 5G/6G edge calculation scene. The method comprises the following steps: in the first stage, aiming at a plurality of dynamic edge servers, optimizing and outputting an equipment distribution scheme through an operation genetic algorithm; the second stage is oriented to a single dynamic edge server, through Gibbs sampling, path planning is output in the first step, task scheduling is output in the second step, and loop iteration replacement is carried out between the first step and the second step so as to find an optimal solution. Aiming at the problems of over-dense deployment and over-high expenditure of the edge server, the invention provides a deployment framework, a joint optimization model and a two-stage optimization algorithm of the mobile edge, establishes parallel execution between motion and operation of the edge server, reduces the expenditure of the deployment server and improves the re

title: Artificial intelligence for predicting 5G network performance
abstract: Computing environments employing Artificial Intelligence (AI) are disclosed for enabling network operators to optimize messaging performance, particularly 5G (and future 6G) messaging performance, in real-time. For example, AI structures can assist in the selection and optimization of modulation tables. Three development phases are described: network data acquisition including faults experienced under various network conditions, AI structure tuning for accurate prediction of performance, and implementation of an algorithm based on the AI structure. Network operators can use the algorithm to compare predicted performance metrics according to various operating conditions, such as available modulation tables, and thereby select operating parameters for improved message reliability and throughput. The algorithm can also be used to adjust network variables, such as particular amplitude or phase levels in modulation tables.

title: Asymmetric modulation for high-reliability 5G communications
abstract: Systems and methods are disclosed for modulating wireless signals in 5G (and future 6G) networks, to mitigate faults from noise or interference, optimize reliability, and enhance message throughput, according to some embodiments. Versions may include modulation tables for phase and amplitude modulation of message symbols in which the number of amplitude levels is different from the number of phase levels. Alternatively, the number of amplitude levels and/or phase levels may be other than a power of two. Other versions may provide a non-uniform spacing of amplitude levels or phase levels for optimal SNR throughout. Specific modulation states of the modulation table may be excluded, or declared invalid for signaling, thereby increasing the noise immunity of the remaining valid states. title: METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR SIGNAL PROCESSING IN A WIRELESS COMMUNICATION SYSTEM
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5G communication system or a 6G communication system for supporting higher data rates beyond a 4G communication system such as long term evolution (LTE). The present disclosure provides a device in a wireless communication system and a method performed by the device. The method comprises: for a first transmitted signal transmitted by the device and a first received signal corresponding to the first transmitted signal and received by the device, compensating one of the first transmitted signal and the first received signal, according to a first synchronization delay part of a synchronization delay between a receiver and a transmitter of the device, wherein the first synchronization delay part is an integral multiple of a predefined baseband sampling interval of the device in the synchronization delay; determining a second synchronization delay part of the synchronization delay based on one of a collection of the first received signal and the compensat

title: FACILITATING A TRANSMISSION POWER DEPENDENT RESOURCE RESERVATION PROTOCOL IN ADVANCED NETWORKS
abstract: Facilitating a transmission power dependent resource reservation protocol in advanced networks (e.g., 5G, 6G, and beyond) is provided herein. Operations of a method can comprise defining, by a system comprising a memory and a processor, a resource reservation procedure that associates respective amounts of reserved resources available for the mobile device based on a transmission power level of the mobile device. The method also can comprise selecting, by the system, an amount of reserved resources from the respective amounts of reserved resources available based on the transmission power level of the mobile device.

title: Mobile terminal with compact antenna pair
assignee: KUNSHAN RUIXIANG XUNTONG COMM TECH CO LTD
abstract: The invention provides a mobile terminal with a compact antenna pair. The mobile terminal comprises a floor, a metal frame and an antenna unit, wherein the inner side of any edge of the metal frame is provided with an open slot, one end of the open slot is provided with a gap, and the open slot is provided with a first feed strip; the antenna unit is arranged close to the open slot; the antenna unit comprises an antenna panel, a second feed strip and a grounding strip; the antenna panel is rectangular and is provided with a groove on the side close to the open slot; the second feed strip and the grounding strip are respectively located at the tail end of the groove and support the antenna panel to be suspended; and the edge, which is provided with the open slot, of the metal frame and the antenna unit form an antenna pair. assignee: JIANGSU YIHENG SPACE TECH CO LTD
abstract: The invention provides a communication signal reconstruction method, which comprises the following steps of: monitoring the communication quality between a mobile terminal and a base station, if the communication quality does not meet a preset condition, carrying out functional region division on a reconfigurable intelligent surface, and determining a corresponding reconstruction mode according to the service type of the mobile terminal; and adjusting a wave beam transmitted by a base station by using the function reconstruction area according to the reconstruction mode, and transmitting the adjusted wave beam to a mobile terminal; the reconfigurable intelligent surface comprises a plurality of sub-arrays, each sub-array serves as an independent functional area division unit, each sub-array comprises N * M information electromagnetic super-structure units, N is larger than or equal to 2, and M is larger than or equal to 2. title: Dual-frequency MIMO antenna assembly and mobile terminal
assignee: KUNSHAN RUIXIANG XUNTONG COMM TECH CO LTD
abstract: The invention provides a dual-frequency MIMO antenna assembly and a mobile terminal. The dual-frequency MIMO antenna assembly comprises a rectangular floor and a dielectric frame arranged around the edge of the floor. MIMO antenna units are arranged on the outer sides of the two ends of each of the four edges of the dielectric frame, and the MIMO antenna units on the two sides of each corner of the four corners of the dielectric frame are distributed in an axial symmetry mode along the boundary line of the two edges forming the corner. According to the dual-frequency MIMO antenna assembly, the eight MIMO antenna units are symmetrically arranged at the four corners of the dielectric frame, so that the dual-frequency MIMO antenna assembly not only has relatively good radiation performance in a Sub-6G frequency band range, but also has relatively high isolation, and the problem of how to enable a Sub-6G antenna to perform dual-frequency radiation and ensure the isolation of the Sub-6G ant

title: Communication method and device
abstract: The invention provides a communication method and device, and can solve the problem that a terminal device frequently receives configuration information of a reference signal so as to reduce the power consumption of the terminal device. The method and device can be applied to a 4G system, a 5G system and a future communication system, such as a 6G system. The method comprises the following steps that terminal equipment receives indication information which is from network equipment and is used for indicating that configuration information of a reference signal is changed, and then determines whether to receive a first message comprising the configuration information of the reference signal according to the indication information, that is, the terminal equipment can determine whether to receive the first message according to the indication information and the own demand, thereby reducing the power consumption of the terminal equipment.

title: Single-sided feeder line ultra-wideband MIMO (Multiple Input Multiple Output) antenna
assignee: JIANGSU JIAHUA COMMUNICATION TECH CO LTD
abstract: The invention provides a single-sided feeder line ultra-wideband MIMO antenna, and the antenna comprises a terminal metal housing which is internally provided with a rectangular accommodation groove; a plastic bracket which is positioned in the rectangular accommodating groove and is connected with the terminal metal shell through a screw; a PCB substrate which is connected with the plastic bracket through double faced adhesive tape, wherein a first antenna unit, a second antenna unit and a third antenna unit are arranged on the face, away from the plastic bracket, of the PCB substrate, and a fourth antenna unit, a fifth antenna unit and a sixth antenna unit are arranged on the face, close to the plastic bracket, of the PCB substrate. title: SELF-LUMINOUS DEVICE, LIQUID CRYSTAL DISPLAY DEVICE, AND MANUFACTURING METHOD FOR SELF-LUMINOUS DEVICE
abstract: The purpose of the present disclosure is to suppress yield decreases caused by defects or mounting defects of LED elements in a self-luminous device. Provided is a self-luminous device (100), wherein each pixel (3) includes, as sub-pixels (51, 52), one basic cell (51) and at least one redundant cell (52). The redundant cell (52) includes an LED element (6R, 6G, 6B) that emits light of the same color as at least one LED element of the LED elements (6R, 6G, 6B) of the basic cell (51). The plurality of sub-pixels (51, 52) of each pixel (3) are configured as a sub-pixel group (50) that is an integrated body constituted by a plurality of LED elements (6R, 6G, 6B) that are integrated. An arrangement pitch (D1) of the sub-pixels (51, 52) in the sub-pixel group (50) is smaller than arrangement pitches (D2, D3) of the sub-pixels (51, 52) in adjacent multiple sub-pixel groups (50).

title: METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR UE ASSISTANCE INFORMATION REPORT FOR SIDELINK DRX IN A WIRELESS COMMUNICATION SYSTEM
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5G communication system or a 6G communication system for supporting higher data rates beyond a 4G communication system such as long term evolution (LTE). A method of base station (BS) in a wireless communication system is provided. The method comprises generating a first indicator requesting a report of a set of sidelink (SL) discontinuous reception (DRX) configurations for SL communications among user equipments (UEs); transmitting a downlink signal including the first indicator; receiving an uplink signal including the report of the set of the SL DRX configurations based on the first indicator indicating that the report of the set of the SL DRX configurations is requested; and configuring, based on the report of the set of the SL DRX configurations, a network DRX configuration for a Uu interface between a UE and a network entity including the BS.

title: DEVICE CONTEXTS, OPERATIONAL MODES, AND POLICY DRIVEN ENHANCEMENTS FOR PAGING IN ADVANCED NETWORKS
abstract: Facilitating device contexts, operational modes, and policy driven enhancements for paging in advanced networks (e.g., 4G, 5G, 6G, and beyond) is provided herein. Operations of a network device can comprise analyzing a group of contextual data for a user equipment device, and mobility management behaviors historically implemented for the user equipment device and based on an indication that a page request is scheduled to be sent to the user equipment device. The operations can also comprise configuring a paging message for the user equipment device based on the contextual data and the mobility management behaviors. Further, the operations can comprise sending the paging message to the user equipment device.

title: WIFI6E antenna
assignee: SHENZHEN ZHONGTIAN COMMUNICATION TECH SHARES CO LTD

According to the utility model, the design is reasonable, the principle is simple, the bandwidth is improved to 5150-7125MHz under the condition that the high frequency of the existing antenna is only 5150-5850MHz, the requirement of the WIFI6E frequency band is met, and the antenna is suitable for various inte

title: Channel estimation method based on short packet communication transmission process
abstract: The invention discloses a channel estimation method based on a short packet communication transmission process, relates to the technical field of communication, and aims to solve the problems of poor transmission reliability and poor effectiveness caused by the fact that a training period must be kept small when a data packet is short in the prior art. Based on a frequency selective channel interference reduction scheme and a virtual pilot frequency and pilot frequency combined channel estimation scheme, the overhead of a frame structure is reduced, and fewer pilot frequencies are assisted to improve the channel estimation quality. The estimation method of the frequency selective channel based on the small data packet further improves the transmission reliability and effectiveness. According to the channel estimation scheme under the frequency selection channel, on the basis of a traditional MMSE channel estimation method, the multi-user interference problem and the frequency selection

title: COMMUNICATION METHOD AND APPARATUS
abstract: Provided are a communication method and apparatus, which can solve the problem of a terminal device frequently receiving configuration information of a reference signal, thereby reducing the power consumption of the terminal device. The method and apparatus can be applied to a 4G system, a 5G system and future communication systems, such as a 6G system. The method comprises: a terminal device receiving indication information which is from a network device and is used for indicating a change in configuration information of a reference signal, and then determining, according to the indication information, whether to receive a first message that comprises the configuration information of the reference signal. That is, the terminal device can determine, according to the indication information and its own requirement, whether to receive the first message, such that the power consumption of the terminal device can be reduced.

title: CELLULAR SYSTEM
abstract: A system includes a housing with one or more edge processors to handle processing on behalf of a mobile target or to provide local data to the mobile target or to provide artificial intelligence for the mobile target; one or more antennas coupled to the housing; and a processor to control a directionality of the antennas in communication with the mobile target using 5G or 6G protocols.

title: 5G/6G ORAN STAR ARRAY WITH SIMULTANEOUS TRANSMIT AND RECEIVE
abstract: A system and method for providing interference cancellation for simultaneous transmit and receive are presented. In one embodiment, the system includes an analog stage including a transmit chain receiving a first digital transmit signal and providing an analog transmit signal output, an auxiliary transmit chain receiving a second digital transmit signal and providing an analog interference cancellation signal output, and a receive chain receiving an analog receive signal input and the analog interference cancelation signal and providing a digital receive signal output; and a digital stage in communication with the analog stage, and including a cancellation system receiving a digital transmit signal and providing the first digital transmit signal and the second digital transmit signal and receiving the digital receive signal and providing a filtered receive signal.

title: 6G ORAN STAR Array With Simultaneous Transmit And Receive
abstract: A system and method for providing interference cancellation for simultaneous transmit and receive are presented. In one embodiment, the system includes an analog stage including a transmit chain receiving a first digital transmit signal and providing an analog transmit signal output, an auxiliary transmit chain receiving a second digital transmit signal and providing an analog interference cancellation signal output, and a receive chain receiving an analog receive signal input and the analog interference cancellation signal and providing a digital receive signal output; and a digital stage in communication with the analog stage, and including a cancellation system receiving a digital transmit signal and providing the first digital transmit signal and the second digital transmit signal and receiving the digital receive signal and providing a filtered receive signal.

title: Unmanned aerial vehicle geometric model building method based on intelligent reflecting surface assistance
abstract: The invention discloses an unmanned aerial vehicle geometric model establishment method based on intelligent reflecting surface assistance, which comprises the following steps: establishing an unmanned aerial vehicle geometric model based on intelligent reflecting surface assistance according to the position relationship among an unmanned aerial vehicle, an intelligent reflecting surface and a receiving end, and obtaining the complex channel gain of a channel; according to the geometric model of the unmanned aerial vehicle, designing an optimization problem according to a received signal power maximization principle; the optimization problem is simplified; solving an optimal reflection phase of the intelligent reflection surface; time-varying parameters among the unmanned aerial vehicle, the user side and the intelligent reflecting surface are determined; and solving a space-time correlation function based on the assistance of the intelligent reflecting surface through the obtained ref

title: METHOD, SYSTEM, AND APPARATUS FOR SHARING DYNAMIC FREQUENCY IN MOBILE COMMUNICATION SYSTEM
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a communication method and system for converging a 5th-Generation (5G) communication system or a 6th-Generation (6G) communication system for supporting higher data rates beyond a 4th-Generation (4G) system. Provided is a method, system, and apparatus for sharing a dynamic frequency in a wireless communication system. A method, performed by a network entity, of assigning a frequency channel, includes: receiving a frequency channel assignment request from a first device; identifying a device cluster including the first device; and assigning, to the first device, at least one frequency channel included in a frequency channel set assigned to the device cluster including the first device. The device cluster may satisfy a certain condition and may be a set of a plurality of devices sharing at least one frequency channel included in the frequency channel set.

title: METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR ALLOCATING FREQUENCY RESOURCES IN WIRELESS COMMUNICATION SYSTEM
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting data transmission rates higher than that of a 4G communication system such as LTE. Disclosed are a method and an apparatus for indicating a frequency resource or a format of the resource in a wireless communication system using a dual connection scheme. The method and the apparatus of the present disclosure semi-statically or dynamically change: a method for classifying frequency resources as resource block sets or resource group units and configuring at least one from among uplink, downlink, and flexible frequency resource formats for a resource group; and the configured frequency resource format, and thus the frequency resources for uplink/downlink transmission and reception can be effectively used.

title: METHOD, SYSTEM, AND APPARATUS FOR SHARING DYNAMIC FREQUENCY IN MOBILE COMMUNICATION SYSTEM
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a communication method and system for converging a 5th-Generation (5G) communication system or a 6th-Generation (6G) communication system for supporting higher data rates beyond a 4th-Generation (4G) system.Provided is a method, system, and apparatus for sharing a dynamic frequency in a wireless communication system. title: METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR FREQUENCY DOMAIN RESOURCE ALLOCATION IN WIRELESS COMMUNICATION SYSTEM
abstract: The present invention relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data rate after a 4G communication system such as LTE. Disclosed are a method and an apparatus for indicating a frequency resource or a format of the resource in a wireless communication system using a dual access scheme. The method and the apparatus of the present invention divide the frequency resource into a resource block set or a resource group unit, set at least one frequency resource format of a downlink, uplink, or flexible resource format for the resource group, change the set frequency resource format to a semi-static or dynamic frequency resource format, and effectively use frequency resources in uplink/downlink transmission and reception.

According to the antenna assembly provided by the utility model, the breakpoints are arranged on the metal frame body, and the metal frame body of the mobile equipment is used as a radiator of the antenna, so that simultaneous integration of a Sub-6G antenna, a WIFI 2.4 G + 5G two-in-one antenna, an LTE

title: Control method and system for randomly filling unmanned aerial vehicle device based on ecological geographic algorithm
assignee: NANJING KANGBO SMART HEALTH RES INSTITUTE CO LTD
abstract: The invention provides a control method for randomly filling an unmanned aerial vehicle device based on an ecogeography algorithm, and the method comprises the steps: obtaining at least two target control parameters for generating an actual 6G satellite global shape from a plurality of base stations built in a target region; performing hybrid calculation on each target control parameter based on a semi-variance hybrid algorithm; judging whether each target control parameter meets an end condition of an eco-geographic algorithm or not; performing mixed processing on each target control parameter through an ecological geographic algorithm, and turning to the second step; and importing each target control parameter into an ecogeography algorithm to optimize the shape of the random electric wave superposition tower. title: METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR SHARING FREQUENCY RESOURCES BETWEEN MOBILE COMMUNICATION PROVIDERS IN WIRELESS COMMUNICATION SYSTEM
abstract: The disclosure relates to a communication method and system for converging a 5th-Generation (5G) communication system or a 6th-Generation (6G) communication system for supporting higher data rates beyond a 4th-Generation (4G) system. A method of communicating with a user equipment by using a frequency resource of a second mobile communication provider in a mobile communication system, the method being performed by a first base station of a first mobile communication provider is provided. The method includes requesting to permit the first base station to use a second frequency resource of the second mobile communication provider, transmitting, to the user equipment, setting information, for establishing communication using the second frequency resource of the second mobile communication provider, through a first frequency resource of the first mobile communication provider, and communicating with the user equipment through the second frequency resource, based on the setting information.

title: Film-Stretching Apparatus And Method Of Producing Film
abstract: A film-stretching apparatus (5) in accordance with an embodiment of the present invention includes a stretching furnace (6) which is divided into a plurality of air amount control zones (6a-6g). In acase where the number of the plurality of air amount control zones is seven, a total amount of air to be discharged from, out of the plurality of air amount control zones, three air amount control zones (6a-6c) which are located on an entrance side (61) of the stretching furnace is controlled so as to be larger than a total amount of air to be discharged from, out of the plurality of air amount control zones (6e-6g), three air amount control zones which are located on an exit side (62) of the stretching furnace.

title: METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR DISCONTINUOUS RECEPTION OPERATIONS OF SIDELINK GROUPCAST/BROADCAST
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5G communication system or a 6G communication system for supporting higher data rates beyond a 4G communication system such as long term evolution (LTE). Methods and apparatuses in a wireless communication system. A method of operating a first UE includes: determining whether to initiate a SL DRX operation for a SL groupcast/broadcast operation; identifying at least one of a SL DRX cycle length, a SL DRX start offset, and SL DRX timers for the SL groupcast/broadcast operation; identifying, based on at least one of a destination ID and the SL DRX start offset, a time instance in which a SL DRX cycle starts, wherein the time instance comprises at least one of a slot, a subframe, and a frame; and receiving, from a second UE belongs to the destination ID, a PSCCH and a PSSCH based on the at least one of the SL DRX cycle length, the SL DRX start offset, the destination ID, and the SL DRX timers.

title: COMPUTING WORKLOAD MANAGEMENT IN NEXT GENERATION CELLULAR NETWORKS
abstract: Various embodiments generally may relate to the field of wireless communications. For example, some embodiments may relate to enabling augmented computing as a service or network capability for sixth-generation (6G) networks. For example, some embodiments may be directed to the operation of a compute control client (Comp CC) at the UE side, and a compute control function (Comp CF) and compute service function (Comp SF) at the network side, which are referred to herein as "compute plane" functions to handle computing related control and user traffic.

title: PHASE TRACKING REFERENCE SIGNAL FOR 6G WIRELESS COMMUNICATION
abstract: An apparatus and system to compensate for phase noise in a 5G or 6G DFT-S-OFDM signal are described. An access port (AP)-specific orthogonal cover code (OCC) is applied to phase tracking reference signal (PTRS) symbols in each of a plurality of PTRS groups. The PTRS group are inserted between data symbols to form a data vector prior to perform transform precoding, on the data vector and transmission to a UE. The UE extracts the PTRS symbols from different PTRS APs using the OCC specific to each AP. After extraction, the phase noise for each PTRS group is estimated and used to compensate the data symbols associated with the PTRS group.

title: FACILITATING DYNAMIC SATELLITE AND MOBILITY CONVERGENCE FOR MOBILITY BACKHAUL IN ADVANCED NETWORKS
abstract: Facilitating dynamic satellite and mobility convergence for mobility backhaul in advanced networks (e.g., 4G, 5G, 6G and beyond) is provided herein. Operations of a system can comprise determining that a group of user equipment devices are located in a defined geographic area and are consuming more than a defined level of resources of a wireless communications network based on an amount of network traffic received from the group of user equipment devices. The operations also can comprise configuring an integrated network comprising a first group of terrestrial network devices and a second group of satellite network devices. Further, the operations can comprise routing at least a portion of network traffic associated with the group of user equipment devices among the first group of terrestrial network devices and the second group of satellite network devices.

title: 6G communication network-oriented super-large-scale spectrum knowledge graph construction method
abstract: The invention discloses a 6G communication network-oriented super-large-scale spectrum knowledge graph construction method. From the perspective of wireless communication, a framework is provided for realizing a core technology of intelligent spectrum resource management and control, and a thought is provided for constructing a domain knowledge graph in the field of wireless communication. title: Heterogeneous unmanned aerial vehicle network switching parameter setting method under 6G
abstract: The invention provides a heterogeneous unmanned aerial vehicle network switching parameter setting method under 6G. The multi-layer unmanned aerial vehicle network can meet more complex user requirements, and switching parameters need to be set meticulously in order to guarantee service continuity. The method comprises the following steps: establishing a distance equivalent model between the unmanned aerial vehicle base station and a user, equivalently calculating the horizontal distance between any layer of unmanned aerial vehicle base station and the user when the unmanned aerial vehicle base station meets switching, switching failure and ping-pong conditions by applying the equivalent model, and setting an optimal switching parameter by taking the switching failure probability and the ping-pong effect probability as indexes. title: Sub-6G-LTCC radio frequency front-end microsystem module
assignee: ANHUI LANXUN COMMUNICATION TECH CO LTD
abstract: The invention discloses a Sub-6G-LTCC radio frequency front-end microsystem module. The Sub-6G-LTCC radio frequency front-end microsystem module is characterized by comprising an antenna module, a radio frequency circuit module and a power supply module. The radio frequency circuit module comprises a low noise amplifier, a frequency mixing module, an intermediate frequency filter and an intermediate frequency room amplifier which are connected in sequence; the low noise amplifier is used for amplifying a radio frequency signal, and the frequency mixing module is used for reducing the radio frequency signal amplified by the low noise amplifier to intermediate frequency; the intermediate frequency filter processes and filters the generated intermediate-frequency signal to obtain a clean intermediate-frequency signal. title: Power control factor setting method for 6G unmanned aerial vehicle user
abstract: The invention provides a power control factor setting method for a 6G unmanned aerial vehicle user. In the method, an unmanned aerial vehicle user connected to a ground network and an uplink transmission power control scene of a ground user are considered, the mobility of the unmanned aerial vehicle user is considered, and the movement of the unmanned aerial vehicle user within a certain distance from a connected base station is considered, so that a movement area is a hemisphere with a certain radius by taking the base station as a center; a random walk model is applied to represent the randomness of movement, a partial path loss reverse power control method is applied, the network comprehensive coverage rate of the unmanned aerial vehicle user and the ground user is used as a key index, and the coverage rate of different power control factors of the ground user and the unmanned aerial vehicle user under certain network configuration is calculated through an estimation algorithm. abstract: The invention discloses a Sub-6G-LTCC high-frequency radio frequency cable protection assembly, which comprises a fixed plate, a connector and a cable. title: Sub-6G-LTCC high-frequency radio-frequency anti-rotation cable assembly
assignee: ANHUI LANYU ELECTRONIC TECH CO LTD
abstract: The invention discloses a Sub-6G-LTCC high-frequency radio-frequency anti-rotation cable assembly, and relates to the technical field of radio-frequency cables. The Sub-6G-LTCC high-frequency radio-frequency anti-rotation cable assembly comprises at least one wire, and the outer surface of the wire is wrapped with a cable sleeve; and a cable anti-rotation assembly is arranged on the outer surface of the cable sleeve. title: METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR DETERMINATION OF UPLINK/DOWNLINK TRANSPORT BLOCK SIZE AND MODULATION AND CODING SCHEME
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5th generation (5G) communication system or a 6th generation (6G) communication system for supporting higher data rates beyond a 4th generation (4G) communication system such as long term evolution (LTE). A non-terrestrial network (NTN) node transmits configuration information including an indication of a maximum transmission block size (TBS) for a physical uplink shared channel (PUSCH) transmission or a physical downlink shared channel (PDSCH). A TBS for the PUSCH or PDSCH is determined based on at least the indicated TBS, and one of a signal indicates one of a maximum modulation and coding scheme (MCS) for the PUSCH or PDSCH transmission or whether the PUSCH or PDSCH transmission uses a default MCS, or the MCS is determined based on at least the indicated maximum MCS, and the PUSCH is transmitted or the PDSCH is received based on the determined TBS and the determined MCS. title: LATENCY REDUCTION IN 5G AND 6G NETWORKS
abstract: An apparatus and system for reducing URLLC latency are described. A single packet mode is described in which, when a packet arrives at the PDCP layer for transmission, an indication is sent to a scheduler with the expected size of the packet after ciphering. The packet is then sent to the RLC layer and concurrently submitted to the ciphering engine to enable ciphering to occur in parallel with the other activities. The scheduler determines the expected TB size for transmission of the packet by determining the size of the PDCP, MAC, and RLC headers. The scheduler matches the resource allocation for transmission to the expected TB size.

title: FACILITATING SPARSITY ADAPTIVE FEEDBACK IN THE DELAY DOPPLER DOMAIN IN ADVANCED NETWORKS
abstract: Facilitating sparsity adaptive feedback in the delay doppler domain in advanced networks (e.g., 4G, 5G, 6G, and beyond) is provided herein. Operations of a method can comprise determining, by a first device comprising a processor, a channel covariance matrix in a time-frequency domain based on a channel estimation associated with reference signals received from a second device. The method also can comprise decomposing, by the first device, the channel covariance matrix into a group of component matrices. Further, the method can comprise transforming, by the first device, respective matrices of the group of component matrices into respective covariance matrices in a delay doppler domain. The method also can comprise determining, by the first device, channel state information feedback in the delay doppler domain.

title: Radio frequency system design method suitable for Sub-6G
assignee: ANHUI LAN MAIN COMMUNICATION CO LTD
abstract: The invention relates to a radio frequency system, in particular to a radio frequency system design method suitable for Sub-6G, which comprises circuit board design, shielding structure design, power supply filtering design and radio frequency circuit design, in the radio frequency circuit design, a radio frequency circuit and a digital circuit are arranged in different areas, and the inseparable digital circuits are divided into the radio frequency circuit. abstract: The present application provides an access network management and configuration method, system and apparatus. title: SIGNALING AND TRIGGER MECHANISMS FOR HANDOVER
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5G communication system or a 6G communication system for supporting higher data rates beyond a 4G communication system such as long term evolution (LTE). Methods and apparatuses for signaling and trigger mechanisms for handover in a wireless communication system. A method for operating a user equipment (UE) includes receiving system information and information corresponding to different classes for handover signaling; transmitting a capability information including information indicating support of enhanced signaling mechanisms; receiving configuration information including measurement information and handover information based on the capability information; determining completion of handover based on the configuration information; and transmitting a completion of handover indication using a non-Radio Resource Control mechanism based on the completion of handover determination.

title: METHOD AND APPARATUS TO TRANSMIT AND RECEIVE SIGNAL FOR GROUPCAST IN WIRELESS COMMUNICATION SYSTEM
abstract: The disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting higher data transmission rates than 4G communication systems such as LTE systems. A method performed by a base station in a wireless communication system is provided. The method includes generating a signal including configuration information for at least one bandwidth part (BWP) and transmitting the generated signal. At least part of the at least one BWP may be configured as a resource for multicast.

title: METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR RADIO RESOURCE MANAGEMENT AND SPECTRUM COORDINATION IN A WIRELESS COMMUNICATION SYSTEM
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5G communication system or a 6G communication system for supporting higher data rates beyond a 4G communication system such as long term evolution (LTE). A method performed by a user equipment (UE) in a wireless communication system is provided. The method includes receiving configuration information for measuring interference from a neighboring cell; measuring the interference from the neighboring cell based on the configuration information; computing a change in the measured interference from the neighboring cell based on a current measured interference level and a previous measured interference level; determining, based on at least in part a determination that the change in the measured interference exceeds a first threshold, whether to generate a measurement report; and transmitting the measurement report on an uplink channel.

title: COMPUTE OFFLOAD SERVICES IN 6G SYSTEMS
abstract: Among other things, embodiments of the present disclosure are directed to solutions to enhance the 5GS for supporting session establishment for augmented computing and dynamic workload migration. In particular, some embodiments are directed to supporting session establishment for compute offload services. Other embodiments may be described and/or claimed.

title: METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR TRANSMITTING/RECEIVING SIGNAL FOR GROUPCAST IN WIRELESS COMMUNICATION SYSTEM
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate than a 4G communication system such as LTE. According to various embodiments of the present disclosure, a method performed by a transmission apparatus in a wireless communication system may be provided. The method may comprise the steps of: transmitting, via a physical downlink control channel (PDCCH), first control information for scheduling a group common-physical downlink shared channel (GC-PDSCH) via which multicast data is transmitted, including information related to hybrid automatic repeat request (HARQ) feedback for the multicast data; and transmitting, via the PDCCH, second control information for scheduling a physical downlink shared channel (PDSCH) via which unicast data is transmitted, including information related to HARQ feedback for the unicast data.

title: METHOD AND APPARATUS TO TRANSMIT AND RECEIVE SIGNAL FOR GROUPCAST IN WIRELESS COMMUNICATION SYSTEM
abstract: The disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting higher data transmission rates than 4G communication systems such as LTE systems. A method performed by a base station in a wireless communication system is provided. The method includes generating a signal including configuration information for at least one bandwidth part (BWP) and transmitting the generated signal. At least part of the at least one BWP may be configured as a resource for multicast.

A method and an apparatus for transmitting and receiving signals in a frequency-asynchronous non-orthogonal multiple access (FA-NOMA) communication system are provided. The method for transmitting a signal by a base station in an FA-NOMA system includes determining a frequency offset to be applied to a first signal transmitted to a first user equipment (UE) based on a second signal transmitted to a second UE, determining information for interference caused by the frequency offset, determining at least one pre-processing factor for the first UE based on the information for the interference, and transmitting a third signal including at least one first pilot to the first UE using the at least one pre-processing factor. abstract: The present invention relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rate after a 4G communication system such as LTE. title: Method and apparatus for channel estimation in a frequency-asynchronous non-orthogonal multiple access system
abstract: The present invention relates to a fifth-generation (5G) or sixth-generation (6G) communication system for supporting a higher data transfer rate after a fourth-generation (4G) communication system such as long-term evolution (LTE) and, more specifically, to a method and apparatus for estimating a channel in a wireless communication system supporting a frequency-asynchronous non orthogonal multiple access (FA-NOMA) using a frequency offset. title: / METHOD AND APPARATUS TO TRANSMIT AND RECEIVE SIGNAL FOR GROUPCAST IN WIRELSS COMMUNICATION SYSTEM
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transfer rate than a 4G communication system such as LTE. In the present disclosure, a transmitting apparatus in a wireless communication system generates a signal including configuration information for groupcast and transmits a signal including the configuration information for groupcast, wherein the configuration information for groupcast includes bandwidth part (BWP) information for groupcast, and a BWP for groupcast is an initial BWP or a BWP different from the initial BWP.

title: Exchange of time of day between two IoT devices
abstract: The rise of the connected objects known as the “Internet of Things” (IoT) will rival past technological marvels. This application discloses a novel time of day (TOD) acquisition by a first Internet of Things (IoT) device from a second IoT device that possesses accurate time of day in a smart environment. The second IoT device obtains time of day from GPS receiver, 5G/6G IoT network, or WiFi IoT network using IEEE1588 PTP or unused subcarriers of 5G/6G/WiFi IoT network. Second IoT device regularly updates its time of day.

title: APPARATUS AND METHOD FOR PROVIDING CONFIGURATION OF TIME SYNCRONIZATION IN MOBILE NETWORK
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting a higher data transmission rates after a 4G communication system such as an LTE. The present disclosure comprises: a process of receiving a first announce message from a first TSN node having a first grand master (GM) as a clock source; a process of setting a role of the first network entity based on the received first announce message; and a process of transmitting the first announce message comprising the information indicating the set role of the first network entity to a second network entity in a broadcast method. Therefore, the present invention is capable of supporting vision-sensitive communication for more applications.

title: METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR LINK BUDGET ENHANCEMENTS IN COMMUNICATION SYSTEMS
abstract: The disclosure relates to a 5G communication system or a 6G communication system for supporting higher data rates beyond a 4G communication system such as long term evolution (LTE). A method for operating a user equipment (UE) is provided. The method comprises obtaining configuration information for one or more repetitions for one or more channels of a physical downlink control channel (PDCCH), a physical downlink shared channel (PDSCH), or a physical uplink shared channel (PUSCH), wherein the configuration information comprises a parameter to extend a maximum number of repetitions for the one or more channels; and transmitting or receiving the one or more repetitions according to the configuration information.

title: Data transmission method and device and electronic equipment
abstract: The invention provides a data transmission method, a data transmission device and electronic equipment, relates to the technical field of communication, and solves the problem of how to select proper transmitting power by a wireless node in a 6G network according to actual requirements in related technologies. title: Data transmission method and device and electronic equipment
abstract: The invention provides a data transmission method, a data transmission device and electronic equipment, relates to the technical field of communication, and solves the problem of how to improve the bandwidth utilization rate of a 6G network in related technologies. The method comprises the following steps: receiving perception information reported by each wireless node in at least one wireless node served in a current period; determining theoretical bandwidth information supported by each wireless node according to the identification code reported by each wireless node; determining target bandwidth information of each wireless node in the next period according to the theoretical bandwidth information and network parameters reported by each terminal in the at least one terminal in the coverage area reported by each wireless node; sending configuration information carrying the target bandwidth information to each wireless node, wherein the configuration information is used for indicating

title: Bandwidth selection method and device for distributed network architecture
abstract: The invention discloses a bandwidth selection method and device for a distributed network architecture, which relate to the technical field of communication, are used for reasonably using frequency spectrum resources and improving the utilization rate of network resources in a frequency division duplex (FDD) mode in a 6G distributed network architecture. The method comprises the following steps: in a frequency division duplex (FDD) communication mode, when a 6G terminal performs uplink data transmission, acquiring target information corresponding to the next TTI, wherein the target information comprises information corresponding to target uplink data, and the target uplink data is to-be-sent data of the next TTI; packaging the target information and uplink data corresponding to the current TTI to obtain first uplink packaged data, and sending the first uplink packaged data; when a 6G terminal performs downlink data transmission, analyzing downlink encapsulated data to obtain a bandwidt

title: Wide-band dual-polarized metal antenna
assignee: PULSE SUZHOU WIRELESS PRODUCTS CO LTD
abstract: The utility model relates to a wide-band dual-polarized metal antenna, which comprises a plurality of support members, a balun structure with a central gap and adjacent gaps is enclosed among the support members by the same central shaft, the top end of each support member is connected with an antenna radiation unit, and a feed probe for coupling feed is arranged in the central gap. According to the utility model, the requirements of equipment on dual polarization, wide band and high isolation are met, the antenna is suitable for intelligent equipment such as WIFI, LTE and Sub 6G, the antenna structure is relatively simple, and industrial integrated production is facilitated.

title: MBS MBS METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR PROVIDING MBS SERVICE IN MBS SERVICE AREA
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a 5G or 6G communication system for supporting higher data rates after a 4G communication system such as LTE. A method for an SMF having a multicast/broadcast function to support an MBS service in a wireless communication system according to an embodiment of the present invention comprises: a process of receiving a first message for requesting connection to an MB session for the MBS service through an AMF from a terminal; a process of associating a PDU session of the terminal with the MB session based on the first message; a process of determining whether to maintain a first tunnel associated with the PDU session based on the capability of at least one base station included in an MBS service area; and a process of transmitting a second message requesting to switch the first tunnel to an inactive state to the base station serving the terminal when it is decided not to maintain the first tunnel.
